question: Will Mara Voss's scheduling copilot reach a committed public launch or at least three signed design partners by mid-September 2025?

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

budget_constraint.details: Mara Voss has $40k discretionary launch budget; any spend over $50k requires board approval per seed_investor_board rules

pilot_timeline.details: Pilot reports results in ~3 weeks from prediction date 2025-07-18, so results due ~2025-08-08

horizon_deadline.details: Mid-September 2025 deadline for public launch commitment or three signed design partners

board_decision_visibility.rule: board_decision_record is visible to all board members and CEO

pilot_results_visibility.rule: pilot_results_record is visible to devon_reyes and mara_voss

design_partner_signing_visibility.rule: design_partner_signing_record is visible to mara_voss and the signing company representative

mara_voss.why_consequential: drives design partner acquisition, decides to seek board approval for launch, manages discretionary budget

devon_reyes.why_consequential: controls pilot expansion decision which could become a design partner or provide validation

priya_shah.why_consequential: controls board approval for launch spend over $50k, chairs voting process

unresolved_mechanisms: identity_of_other_two_board_members (member_2, member_3) is unknown; their voting behavior cannot be predicted
exact number of design partners currently in negotiation is unknown
whether Calder Logistics pilot expansion would count as a design partner is undefined
whether public launch commitment requires a specific launch date or just an announced intention is ambiguous
institution seed_investor_board dropped: no decision holders declared
assumptions: board_approval_process requires majority vote of 3-member board
pilot results will be submitted by 2025-08-08 as stated
devon_reyes has sole authority to decide pilot expansion
design partner agreements require only mara_voss's signature on the startup side
public launch commitment means a formal board-approved announcement of a specific launch date

issues_first_pass: process 'board_approval_process' needs at least two states
process 'pilot_expansion_process' needs at least two states
process 'design_partner_acquisition_process' needs at least two states

missing_decisive_elements: No representation of Mara Voss's personal motivation, burnout risk, or competing priorities that could derail her execution
No representation of competitor products or market timing pressure that could force premature launch or partner loss
No representation of technical development status (e.g., alpha/beta milestones, bug counts, integration readiness) which is prerequisite to both launch and partner signings
No representation of legal/regulatory hurdles (e.g., data privacy compliance for scheduling copilot) that could block launch or partner contracts
No representation of cash runway or burn rate — critical for whether Mara can sustain operations to mid-September without additional funding
relabeled_progress_bars: Board approval process is modeled as a linear state machine, but real board decisions often involve informal lobbying, delayed votes, or conditional approvals not captured
Design partner acquisition process has only four states — real pipelines have dozens of prospects, warm leads, and silent drop-offs that are not tracked
Pilot expansion decision is binary (expanded/not expanded) but real pilots often yield partial rollouts, extended trials, or non-expansion that still leads to partnership
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes Mara Voss has full authority over design partner signings, but if partners require their own board approval (common for enterprise deals), the timeline is outside her control
The model treats 'signed' as a terminal state, but a signed design partner could later renege or fail to deliver — the question asks for 'at least three signed' but does not require them to be active
The board approval process has no time constraints — Priya Shah could delay scheduling a meeting indefinitely, making mid-September deadline impossible even if all other conditions are met
direct_human_reaction_encodings: No encoding of Mara Voss's stress level, sleep deprivation, or decision fatigue which typically spike before launch deadlines
No encoding of Priya Shah's personal investment thesis or risk appetite — she might block launch if she wants more traction first
No encoding of Devon Reyes's internal politics at Calder Logistics — his expansion decision may be overruled by his own leadership
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: The model treats 'launch_commitment_record' as a factual event, but public launch commitments are often aspirational PR statements that differ from private board-approved timelines
Design partner signings are modeled as binary events, but companies often announce 'partnerships' that are actually non-binding letters of intent — the model does not distinguish
Board approval is modeled as a clean majority vote, but real boards often give verbal approval subject to conditions that are not captured in the state machine
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No representation of software development lifecycle (e.g., CI/CD pipeline failures, dependency bugs, cloud service outages) that could delay launch
No representation of macroeconomic conditions (e.g., interest rate changes, enterprise software spending freezes) that could freeze partner budgets
No representation of calendar quirks — mid-September 2025 may fall on a weekend or holiday, making 'public launch' ambiguous
No representation of data security incidents or PR crises that could force a pivot or delay
